scope.summary: Plan §30.4 item 7 (CC1): the single-principal admission guard had no test at all; a panic taken while it is held must leave the rightful principal's retry admitted and must not hand the binding to anyone else.
out_of_scope: Any production change. The shipped design already satisfies both directions (parking_lot::Mutex over a sticky binding); this close adds the regression that pins it, and deliberately changes no behaviour.
The other 409 sources in crates/oraclemcp-core/src/http/operator.rs (change-proposal store locks etc.), which were another agent's mid-edit throughout and are a different conflict class.
An end-to-end HTTP-level panic injection. The guard is the unit that decides admit-vs-409; driving a real panic through a live listener would test the framework, not this decision.

entry_points.name: cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --lib a_panic_holding_the_admission_guard
entry_points.trace: 2 tests pass. The regression admits principal-a, panics inside catch_unwind while holding active_principal_key, then asserts (1) principal-a's retry is Ok — not stranded behind 409 — and (2) principal-b is still Err. The companion test pins that the refusal really is HTTP 409 with error=single_principal_active, so a status change cannot silently redefine what 'admitted' means. MUTATION-CHECKED: changing admit to hand the binding to any later principal fails with 'a panic must not release the binding to another principal'.

limitations: Unit-level panic injection at the guard, not a panic driven through a live HTTP request; the item's 'framework panic' is simulated by unwinding while the guard's lock is held, which is the state a handler panic leaves behind.

cargo clippy -p oraclemcp-core --lib --tests could not be run with -D warnings: crates/oraclemcp-db/src/intelligence.rs is another agent's mid-edit whose in-flight get_source trips clippy::too_many_arguments (8/7), failing the dependency build. Verified instead by scanning the clippy output for findings in the touched file (none). That mid-edit will red CI when pushed; reported to the swarm, not fixed here.
Scoped verification only: no workspace-wide run.
